title: Hedyotis pubescens
taxonomy: Hedyotis pubescens is classified under the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder or coffee family. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other species within the family. Hedyotis pubescens is further classified under the genus Hedyotis, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Hedyotis is part of the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes many families of flowering plants. Overall, the classification of Hedyotis pubescens reflects its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.

intro: Hedyotis pubescens, also known as Hedyotis diffusa, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the "bitter tea" or "bitter button" plant. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hedyotis pubescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used to make capsules or tablets, where it is valued for its bitter flavor and purported health benefits.
